description: Use this API to calculate simultaneous unbalances. It must be called once with APIOPT set to 1, once for each of the simultaneous faults to be applied with APIOPT set to 2, and once with APIOPT set to 3. This API routine was first introduced in release 34.1.0. ierr = scmu_2(apiopt, options, rval, cktarg)

parameters.name: apiopt
parameters.description: Mode of operation. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: An array of seven elements specifying options and input values to be used in the current calculation phase. It is ignored when APIOPT is 3 or 4. When APIOPT is 1, OPTIONS(1), OPTIONS(2) and OPTIONS(3) define options to be used in the construction of the sequence admittance matrices. These values are used only when the sequence network setup option is enabled, and activity SEQD is to be automatically executed. (input).

parameters.name: rval
parameters.description: An array of five elements specifying input values used to define the current fault; ignored except when APIOPT is 2. The elements of RVAL are dependent on the value specified for OPTIONS(1). (input).

parameters.name: cktarg
parameters.description: Circuit identifier of branch to be faulted; ignored except when apiopt is 2 and options(1) is 8 or 9. (input; ‘1’ by default).

apiopt.description: Initialize; automatically runs activity SEQD if the sequence network setup option setting is enabled.
Specify an unbalance to be applied; must be called once for each simultaneous unbalance to be imposed.
Solve in the presence of the specified unbalances and terminate.
Remove specified unbalances (cancels any unbalances specified by any preceding APIOPT 2 references, and leaves memory as it is immediately following an APIOPT 1 reference).

options.description: Specify it per additional conditions as below.
When APIOPT=1, dc line and FACTS device option, 0 by default.
Block and ignore.
Convert to constant admittance load.
When APIOPT=2, define the type of unbalance to be applied, no default allowed
First line-to-ground fault.
Second line-to-ground fault.
First line-to-line or line-to-line-to-ground fault.
Second line-to-line or line-to-line-to-ground fault.
One phase closed.
Two phases closed.
Three-phase fault (uses fault types 2 and 4).
Branch unbalance with one end opened.
Branch unbalance with both ends closed.

error_codes.description: terminated due to invalid fault specification or application.
SEQD results are not compatible with the working case.
fault analysis is invalid due to the presence of in-service induction machines that are in the “stalled” or “tripped” state.
bus type code and series element status inconsistencies.
prerequisite requirements for API are not met.
